profiles.notes: Broad English IPA hints only. Significant dialect variation exists (GA vs RP). Not a full phonemic inventory.
Broad Spanish IPA hints only. Castilian vs Latin American variation not modeled here.
Broad French IPA hints only. Nasal vowels marked. Liaison and elision not modeled.
Standard German (Hochdeutsch) broad IPA hints. Auslautverh√§rtung (final obstruent devoicing) applies.
Broad Russian IPA hints only. Vowel reduction in unstressed syllables applies. Palatalization pervasive.
Broad Hindi IPA hints only. Aspirated vs unaspirated and retroflex distinctions marked where known.
Broad Japanese IPA hints. Mora-timed language. Pitch accent varies by dialect (Tokyo pattern noted). Geminate consonants (гБ£) not always shown.
Broad Korean IPA hints. Tense/lax/aspirated stop distinction not fully modeled. Final consonant neutralization applies.
Mandarin (Putonghua) broad IPA hints with tone marks. Tonal language: 4 lexical tones plus neutral tone. Pinyin romanization is the standard reference.
Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) broad IPA hints. Pharyngeal and emphatic consonants marked. Short vowels often absent in orthography. Dialect variation is extensive and not modeled.
Broad Turkish IPA hints. Vowel harmony (front/back, round/unround) is a fundamental feature. Agglutinative morphology not modeled here.
Broad Thai IPA hints. Tonal language: 5 lexical tones. Tones marked on vowels. Initial consonant class interacts with tone. No spaces between words in script.
Broad Portuguese IPA hints. European (EP) and Brazilian (BP) variants differ significantly; these hints approximate EP. Nasal vowels marked where known.
Broad Italian IPA hints. Geminate consonants apply but are not always marked. Standard variety (RP-like Florentine/Roman).
Broad Dutch IPA hints. Velar fricative /x/ and voiced /…£/ are distinctive; shown as x/g here. Standard Dutch (AN - Algemeen Nederlands).
Broad Polish IPA hints. Nasal vowels /…ЫћГ/ /…ФћГ/ before fricatives; palatalised and retroflex distinctions approximated.
Broad Ukrainian IPA hints. Distinct from Russian: /…™/ (–Є) vs /i/ (—Ц), /…¶/ (voiced glottal) vs Russian /g/. Palatalisation pervasive.
Broad Vietnamese IPA hints. Six lexical tones in Northern Vietnamese (Hanoi). Tonal diacritics in orthography map to IPA tone marks. Southern variety differs substantially.
Broad Indonesian IPA hints. Relatively phonemic spelling; stress is predictable. /r/ is typically a trill or flap. Schwa /…Щ/ often appears in unstressed syllables.
Broad Bengali IPA hints. Retroflex consonants marked. Script is Bangla (Beng). Western Bengali (Kolkata) assumed; Bangladeshi variety differs.
Broad Tamil IPA hints. Diglossia: formal (centamil) vs colloquial (koduntamil) differ significantly. Retroflex lateral /…≠/ and rhotic /…ї/ are distinctive phonemes.
Broad Swahili IPA hints. Bantu language with fixed penultimate stress. CV syllable structure predominates. Relatively phonemic Latin orthography.

Broad Navajo (Din√© bizaad) IPA hints. Athabaskan tonal language: high/low tone distinction is phonemic. Nasal vowels, glottal stops, and ejective consonants are distinctive. This is a very minimal hint вАФ consult Navajo language resources for authoritative phonology.

Broad Amharic IPA hints. Semitic language written in Ge'ez/Ethiopic script (Ethi). Geminate consonants are phonemic. Ejective consonants (бМ•/бНХ/бЙЕ/бМ®/бНИ) are distinctive. This is a minimal hint вАФ not a full inventory.

Broad Cherokee IPA hints. Iroquoian language with a pitch accent system. Each syllabary character encodes a CV syllable (75 characters + кЃЭ s). This is a minimal hint вАФ authoritative Cherokee phonology resources should be consulted.

Broad Plains Cree (nƒУhiyawƒУwin) IPA hints. Algonquian language with contrastive vowel length. Written in both Cree syllabics (Cans) and Latin orthography. This is a very minimal hint вАФ consult the Cree Literacy Network for authoritative phonology.

Broad Persian/Farsi IPA hints. Written in Perso-Arabic script with additional letters beyond Arabic. Word-final stress is default. Uvular /q/ and velar fricative /x/ are distinctive. Differs from Dari and Tajik varieties.
Broad Modern Hebrew IPA hints (Israeli standard). Word-final stress predominates. Pharyngeal distinctions largely neutralised in Modern Israeli Hebrew. Unpointed text common; vowels often inferred.

Broad Marathi IPA hints. Indo-Aryan, Devanagari script. Retroflexes and aspirates are phonemic. Nasalisation marked on vowels. Similar to Hindi but distinct vocabulary and some phonological differences.
Broad Malay IPA hints. Latin script (Rumi); Jawi (Arabic) also used but not modeled here. Closely related to Indonesian; mutual intelligibility high. /r/ varies by region (trill vs uvular).

Broad Punjabi IPA hints. Indo-Aryan tonal language: three lexical tones (low, mid, high). Gurmukhi script (Guru) standard in India; Shahmukhi (Arabic) in Pakistan. Aspirated consonants and retroflexes are phonemic.
Broad Telugu IPA hints. Dravidian language with its own script (Telu). Retroflex consonants are phonemic. Vowel length is contrastive. Initial stress default.

Broad Urdu IPA hints. Written in Nastaliq (Arabic script). Very close to Hindi in phonology but distinct vocabulary and orthography. Aspirates and retroflexes phonemic. Short vowels typically omitted in script.

Broad Cantonese (Yue Chinese) IPA hints. Six lexical tones. Written with Traditional Chinese characters (Hant). Stop finals /p/, /t/, /k/ are preserved (unlike Mandarin). Yale romanisation or Jyutping are standard references.

Broad Modern Greek IPA hints. Stress marked in orthography with tonos (ќД). Ancient Greek phonology differs significantly. Pitch accent system of Ancient Greek replaced by stress accent in Modern Greek.
